title: acer hyrcanum subsp. reginae-amaliae
intro: Acer hyrcanum subsp. reginae-amaliae, also known as the Persian maple, is a subspecies of maple tree native to the Caspian region and parts of Asia. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as an antiseptic. Herbal preparations of Acer hyrcanum subsp. reginae-amaliae include teas and infusions made from the leaves and bark, which can be consumed for its medicinal properties.
